per_turn_leaks.student_message: I want to make sure we don't miss anything important. When these episodes happen, does your body react in any other way?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: When I'm having these breathing issues, I get this wheezing sound when I breathe out, it's like a high-pitched whistling noise. And sometimes, especially at night, I wake up feeling really short of breath and my heart is racing a bit.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: I know it can be scary to think about, but do you notice any sweating or feeling faint during these episodes?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: To be honest, the sweating doesn't seem to be too bad, but I have been having some pretty intense night sweats lately. As for feeling faint, yeah, sometimes when I'm really short of breath and my heart is racing, I feel a bit lightheaded, like I might pass out if I stand up too quickly.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Thank you for sharing that. I really appreciate your honesty. Does the discomfort stay in one place or does it spread anywhere?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: It's more of a general feeling all over my body, but when I breathe deeply or cough, I feel this sharp pain on my right side, just under my ribs. It's hard to describe, but it feels like something is pinching me there.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Sometimes when people are going through something like this, they also feel nauseous or have stomach upset. Has that happened to you?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: Actually, yes... I've been feeling a bit queasy and my appetite has been off for the past few days. Even thinking about food makes me feel a bit nauseated, which is weird because I usually love cooking and trying new recipes. It's just been really hard to muster up any interest in eating lately.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Have you had any fevers or night sweats? I know it might seem unrelated, but it helps me understand the full picture.
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: I've been having a low-grade fever, around 38.2 degrees Celsius, and it's been intermittent - sometimes it comes and goes throughout the day, other times it just lingers in the evenings. The night sweats have actually been pretty intense, I'll wake up drenched in sweat every night.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: I hate to ask this, but it's really important — has anyone in your family had serious health problems?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: To be honest, my mom has asthma, and she's always been a bit of a worrier, so I'm probably getting a little anxious about this too! But aside from that, I don't have any other known medical conditions. I did used to get these wheezing episodes when I was younger, around age 8 or 9, but they never really got formally diagnosed. They'd happen whenever I had a cold or exercised too much, and they'd go away on their own after a few days. But now that you mention it, maybe that's something we should look into...

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Before we wrap up, is there anything else you're worried about that you want to share?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response:
I'm not sure if it's relevant, but I did have a bit of a scare when I was younger. My maternal grandmother passed away from lung cancer, and sometimes I worry that maybe I inherited some kind of genetic predisposition for respiratory issues. I know it's probably not the most rational thing to think about, but it's hard not to wonder if there's some connection.

Also, I almost forgot to mention that I had a physical exam just over a year ago, and everything seemed normal at the time. But now that I'm thinking back on it, maybe that's something we should look into...
